skill.name: agentdb-vector-search
skill.description: Implement semantic vector search with AgentDB for document retrieval, similarity matching, RAG workflows, and local vector database operations.
skill.summary: Build AgentDB vector search workflows for semantic retrieval and RAG applications.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command examples, local database paths, and two external project URLs. Review confirms no prompt injection, credential theft, or malicious data exfiltration, but the skill does recommend running remote npm packages with npx and starting an MCP server, which needs a marketplace warning.

medium_findings.title: Remote Package Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs users to run npx agentdb@latest commands. This is legitimate setup guidance, but it executes code resolved from the npm registry at runtime and creates supply-chain exposure.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command strings are explicit and numerous, and npx @latest can execute newly fetched package code. The context is documentation rather than hidden execution, so this is a controlled but real risk.

medium_findings.title: Local Vector Database May Store Sensitive Content
medium_findings.description: The examples store document text, embeddings, and metadata in local AgentDB or SQLite database paths. This is expected behavior for vector search, but users may index private documents without retention or access-control guidance.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The storage examples are clear and intentionally local. The concern is privacy handling for indexed content, not malicious database access.

medium_findings.title: RAG Prompt Construction Needs Injection Controls
medium_findings.description: The RAG example combines retrieved context and a user question into a generated prompt. This is a normal RAG pattern, but untrusted retrieved text can carry prompt injection instructions unless callers add filtering and source controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code shows direct prompt assembly from retrieved context and user input. It is a simplified example, so the risk depends on how implementers harden the production workflow.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Project URLs Are Benign
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the AgentDB GitHub project and website. No evidence found that the URLs receive user data, credentials, embeddings, or database contents.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are listed under a Learn More section and are not used in executable code. This makes the network finding a low-risk documentation reference.

low_findings.title: SQLite Database References Are Expected Storage Paths
low_findings.description: The SQLite and vector database filenames are examples for local storage. No evidence found of credential database targeting or unauthorized file discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The database paths are used as examples for AgentDB initialization, query, and stats commands. They do not reference browser stores, credential files, or system secrets.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Static Signals Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to match ordinary words in the skill description and quantization section. The reconnaissance findings point to normal stats or metadata search examples, not host enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, system inventory, or credential discovery code at these locations. The signals are consistent with false-positive keyword matches.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx @latest Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: Using npx with @latest can execute package code that changes over time. Users should pin versions and review package provenance before running the commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in command examples. It is a standard installation pattern, but it still executes remote package code.

dangerous_patterns.title: MCP Server Setup Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs users to add an AgentDB MCP server through Claude Code. MCP tools can expose local database operations, so users should only enable trusted servers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The MCP setup commands and named tools are explicitly documented. The risk is conditional on user installation and tool permissions.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast semantic retrieval when documents outgrow keyword search. This skill provides AgentDB commands and API patterns for vector storage, similarity search, and RAG workflows.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to initialize AgentDB vector databases with dimensions and presets.
Provides query examples for cosine, Euclidean, and dot-product similarity search.
Demonstrates TypeScript patterns for storing text, embeddings, and metadata.
Explains RAG retrieval, MMR result diversity, quantization, caching, and HNSW indexing.
Includes MCP server setup commands for Claude Code AgentDB tools.
Lists troubleshooting commands for performance, memory use, relevance, and dimensions.
limitations: It is a guidance file and does not include runnable project source files.
It depends on AgentDB, agentic-flow, Node.js, and an embedding provider.
It does not provide production access controls, retention policies, or encryption guidance.
Benchmark claims should be verified in the user's own workload and hardware.

use_cases.title: Prototype a RAG Retriever
use_cases.description: Use AgentDB examples to store document embeddings and retrieve relevant context for question answering.

use_cases.title: Tune Vector Search Performance
use_cases.description: Compare distance metrics, quantization choices, thresholds, and batch operations for large document collections.

use_cases.title: Add AgentDB Tools to Claude Code
use_cases.description: Follow the MCP setup pattern to expose AgentDB query, store, and stats operations inside Claude Code.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Vector Database
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB vector search skill to show me the safest steps to initialize a local vector database for OpenAI embeddings.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Similarity Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB vector search skill to design a semantic query flow with top-k results, a similarity threshold, and metadata filters.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a RAG Retrieval Layer
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB vector search skill to plan a RAG retrieval layer that stores documents, computes embeddings, retrieves context, and reduces prompt injection risk.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Large Vector Index
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB vector search skill to compare quantization, HNSW indexing, caching, and batch inserts for a collection with one million vectors.

output_examples.input: I need semantic search for a documentation site.
output_examples.output: A setup plan with embedding dimensions, a local AgentDB database path, metadata fields, query thresholds, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: My vector search is slow and memory usage is high.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist covering stats commands, quantization options, batch loading, cache settings, and relevance threshold changes.

output_examples.input: I want Claude Code to query my local vector store.
output_examples.output: A controlled MCP setup outline that explains the AgentDB server command, expected tools, and trust checks before enabling it.

best_practices: Pin AgentDB package versions before running npx commands in production environments.
Keep private documents and embeddings in approved storage with clear retention rules.
Validate retrieved context before adding it to prompts for RAG generation.
anti_patterns: Do not run npx commands from unreviewed packages on sensitive workstations.
Do not index secrets, credentials, or regulated data without access controls.
Do not trust retrieved text as instructions for the AI assistant.

faq.question: Does this skill include executable source code?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill with command examples and TypeScript usage patterns.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: It targets Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: The examples use npx and external documentation links, so setup may require network access.

faq.question: Can it work with private documents?
faq.answer: Yes, but users should protect the vector database and avoid indexing secrets without policy controls.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill recommends remote package execution and MCP setup. No malicious intent was found, but users should review commands before running them.

faq.question: Does it teach RAG workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows retrieval, context assembly, similarity thresholds, and MMR result diversity.
